Regnum Sardiniae et Corsicae

§RGain 5 infamy§! The Regnum Sardiniae et Corsicae was established by Pope Boniface VIII, who granted the title to James II of Aragon, turning him into the nominal King of Sardinia and Corsica in 1297. Despite that, James II would only start to conquer Sardinia in 1324 and Aragonese rule over the island would be contested until 1420. Not only that, but Aragon would never manage to encroach in the Island of Corsica, letting the inland remain under Genoese and then French control, despite a brief period of independence. \n\nNow, more than 500 years since the kingdom's creation, it is about time time we reclaim ownership over the Kingdom of Sardinia and Corsica and, for the first time, trully unify both islands under our rule.
We have been forced to expatriate the Bourbons. Sadly, we are now obligated to lead our kin elsewhere, well away from the French lands they once ruled.
We have been beaten back the French, preventing them for forcefully expatriating the French Bourbons. They will reside in our realm whether they like it or not!

After a bit of force, we forced the crown in Spain to back down and exile the French Bourbons from their country. Political security has been secured, and the French Bourbons have been forced to move elsewhere, hopefully somewhere further away from the border.

The Crown of Spain has just reaffirmed—through iron and blood—that we can't violate its sovereignty to keep the Bourbon monarchs within their borders, despite our attempts to expatriate them by force. We have little recourse in this matter, save dropping the matter entirely.

After failing to expatriate the Charlex X and his descendents from Spain, we would soon found ourselves at war with Spain, leading to our regretable defeat not long after. The Spanish made sure to take a piece of our core territory in the peace deal, a decision that baffled all but the most insane of observers, but soon the reason would become clear. The territory annexed by Spain became, in practive, the a Bourbon fief, with the descendents of Charles X transforming it into a little Bourbon France. Now, this folly of a king has declared independence from Spain and state his desire to retake his rightful throne, marching on our territory. Now we have no choice but to put an end to the Bourbon menace once and for all.

The idea of moving the nation's capital to Saint Louis isn't a new one. Such an idea has been floating around since the Mexican-American War, with some people seeing the city's central location as perfect for the expanding American nation. After the American Civil War, there were at least three proposed resolutions to move the capital to St. Louis, instead of renovating the expanding District of Columbia. Only the third proposal, by Herbert Paine, as a joke, managed to be put to a vote, where it was defeated by a vote of 77 to 97. After that, the idea was still pushed around by a few minds in the Midwest, but it would ultimately die down not long after.
William I's Ambitions

§RGain 15 infamy§! While the Congress of Vienna saw us grow with the annexation of the Austrian Netherlands and personal union over Luxemburg, William I had the ambition of getting pretty much most of what is now the Prussian Rhineland, connecting the Netherlands to his hereditary lands in Nassau. His ambitions, however, would be set aside, as Prussia as offered the Rhineland and the Netherlands was left at its current size, despite some resistance from William I. Now, with our position as a great power safe, we can consider if reigniting these old ambitions would be worth, considering that we would not only be upseting the balance of power established by the Congress of Vienna, but also poking the hornet's nest that is Germany.
Is Iran
Reaffirm Claims on Bahrain

§RGain 2.5 infamy§! The Bahrain Archipelago was always traditionally tied to Iran, with its population being mostly of Iranian origin, with Iran usually ruling it indirectly, through its emirs in the port of Bushehr. While the Qajars still hold claims to these islands, they haven't been under Iranian overlordship ever since the Zand Dynasty, with the island being ruled by Sunni emirs that have since refused to reconize rule from Tehran, going as far as getting protection from the British to keep themselves free from our rule. \n\nWhile we managed to strike an agreement with the British for the reincorporation of Bahrain, the vile Europeans went back on their word, refusing to return Bahrain to us. It is about time we reaffirm our claims to Bahrain, with no regard to what the British or any other foreign power thinks about it!

Once a center of German culture and politics, now the streets of Frankfurt are lay haunted by gunfire as the French soldiers quell what little resistance is left to their occupation of the city. The $FROMCOUNTRY_ADJ$ soldiers flood the Bundespalais, looking for the Austrian representative to the German Confederation, soon finding out that he has long made his way back to Austria. With the palace deserted, a $FROMCOUNTRY_ADJ$ officer would read orders coming from the $FROMCOUNTRY_ADJ$ capital ordering the burning of the Palais Thurn und Taxis. Slowly the fire would consume the bulding, spreading to the nearby buldings and leading to vivid flames to take over the Frankfurter skyline. \n\nThe Burning of the Bundespalais would strike a great blow to the entire German Confederation, and by carrying out such a grievous act $FROMCOUNTRY$ challenges the Concert of Europe drawn by the diplomats at the Congress of Vienna. Now many of the German leaders sit uneasy not knowing what the future holds for the German world now that the fate of the German Confederation is uncertain.
Burn the Bundespalais

§RGain 5 infamy§! As the center of German politics, Frankfurt sits as one of the most important cities in the German Confederation, together with Berlin and Vienna. By capturing it we could take over the Palais Palais Thurn und Taxis—The Bundespalais—and strike a huge blow against the German world by burning it to the ground, pressing the German Confederation to come to the negotiation table. Doing so would naturally be seen as an challenge to the European order, but it may be worth it...

The Bundespalais burns! Our representatives to the Federal Convention luckily made their way out of the city long before the invading troops reached the city, but that hardly means we are safe. The war still wages on and—with the failture of the German Confederation to protect its own capital—the German nationalists are once again gaining popularity, defending the end of the German Confederation and the creating of a German Empire to better find foreign threats and protect the German people.

Although $COUNTRY$ has tried to control the Italian peninsula dividing it between Savoy-Piedmont, Naples, and the Papal States, the fires of Italian nationalism continue to burn. Many in Paris are of the opinion that a unified Italy is impossible to avoid, and it is best that the Italian state be created peacefully through $COUNTRY_ADJ$ guidance rather through a bloody revolution. Others argued that a unified Italy will quickly grow out of our control. Either way a decision must be reached on what to do with Italy. The question then arises who shall rule the Italy. Should it be Savoyards in the north? Perhaps the House of Savoy rule in Turin will result in a loyal and capable subject? On the other hand, the rulers of Naples were once of French blood, and it could be wise to keep things in the family. There are some who say the crown should go to neither the Savoyards nor the Sicilians, but rather the Holy Father himself in Rome. And of course there are those who think the right course of action is to tighten our grip on Italy.

With our recent treaties with the Gulf States, we managed to create an informal protectorate currently known as "The Trucial States". We hold a loose hand over the states in the Gulf, but the local $COUNTRY_ADJ$ resident in the region suggest we could deepen our relationship now that the Gulf States are completely under our diplomatic control, pushing for a closer relationship between us and them.
Reclaim the Kingdom of Westphalia

§RGain 5 infamy§! Sharing little to no territory with Westphalia proper, the (Napoleonic) Kingdom of Westphalia was established by Napoleon after the Peace of Tilsit in 1807, with it being carved from Prussian and to Napoleon's youngest brother, Jérôme Bonaparte. Westphalia was meant to be a model state to the other states of the Confederation of the Rhine and while it succeed at that to some degree, Jérôme's spending practices soon would alienate him towards his older brother. While the Kingdom of Westphalia was soon dissolved with Napoleon's defeat, now that Westphalia exists once again we are free to claim the territories that it had under the rule of Napoleon.

With the French victory against Prussia in the Rhine Crisis and the rise of German nationalism as the Springtime of Nations rages on, the new Bavarian Minister-President, came to us with the Trias Plan, promoting the idea that we rally Baden and Württemberg to unite into a third major power in Germany, the South German Federation. Seeing as both Austria and Prussia failed to protect the Bundespalais and the Confederation as a whole, it seems like the only reasonable course of action is stepping up to protect our brothers, instead of being subject to the whims of Berlin and Vienna.

In light of the recent events, Bavaria has invited us to unite with them in a federation of South German states to counterbalance Austro-Prussian influence in the region. With the recent wave of nationalist revolutions and the defeats at the hands of the French, this may be the safest cost of action to take.

The German Confederation is dead. After the defeat at the hands of the French and the Burning of Frankfurt,the liberal revolutions that swept the country were too much for the system put in place by Metternich. The Prussians have rallied part of the Confederation around them, with the intent of protecting them against further French agression and eventually revenging the loss of the Rhineland and Frankfurt. Meanwhile closer to home, the Bavarians broke off from the Confederation by forming a federated state with Baden and Württemberg, trying to create a new major power in Germany, straying away from both Prussian and Austrian influence. The Confederation is officially dead, with Metternich's meticulous political framework being replaced by revolutionary winds and the wish for petty revenge.
The Legacy of the Hanseatic Departments

§RGain 5 infamy§! With the Kingdom of Westphalia reclaimed, another artifact of Napoleon's rule calls our attention. The so-called "Hanseatic Departments", a broad classification of the 4 departments created by Napoleon I with his annexation of northeastern Germany, stretching from Münster to Lübeck. While we have no intention to annex these lands well beyond the Rhine, we could use our influence in the region to secure the region once again under a unified protectorate composed of these old territories, securing our hold over the region.

On the other side of the Carpathian Mountains, the Austria announced their ambitions to unify Germany following their victory over Prussia. Following that, they made an effort to relinquish their hold over their old crownlands, restoring an "independent" principality in Transylvania. While such state falls firmly under Austrian influence—preventing its immediate unification with our nation—we could petition the Austrians to sanction such a union across the Carpathian Mountains, letting us integrate most of Transylvania without bloodshed.

Following our withdrawal from Transylvania, the Romanians seem to be interested in unifying their lands with the our old principality, having petitioned to have our leaders sanctioning such a union, lest our countries enter into conflict for a land we aren't particularly interested in. Of course, such a union would come with guarantees to the local—especially German—minorities, to safeguard their rights against the newly-established Romanian nation.

Our delegation to Vienna has been a success! With the Austrians assuring that we have their sanction to unify with Transylvania! While our newly annexed lands won't encompass all of our claims, it is still a victory to our newly-established nation! Of course, there were some stipulations set by the Austrians, such as guarantees to the local minorities in the region, but that is a small price to pay for the territory.
The Austrians have refused to sanction our unification with Transylvania. We will be sure to remember this moment and bide our time until the territory is ripe for the taking...

After Hungary's defeat in the 1848 Hungarian Revolution, the Voivodeship of Serbia and Banat of Temeschwar was formed from the provinces of Banat, Baranja and Batschka. The area was inhabited by a mix of Hungarians, Serbians and—more importantly—Danube Swabians, leading to a significant German population in the area. Upon the independence of our non-German lands we established an independent government in Banat, much to the distaste of the Hungarians. \n\nNow, with our unification of the German states, the Hungarians seem to think we are not interested in maintaining the Free State of Banat's independence, petitioning us to let it be once again annexed by Hungary. Obviously, this has been met with opposition by the Germans of Banats, which instead ask that we rebuke any Hungarian ambitions in the region.
Restore Hungarian Claims

The Kingdom of Hungary was a huge state spanning from the border of the Holy Roman Empire to the Carpathians. Sadly, in our last years under Habsburg rule our Kingdom was stripped of its historical claims. Now that we are fully independent and free from Habsburg influence it is finally time we restore our old claims.

Following some recent victories in the Eastern Front, the old Taurida Governate of the Russian Empire came under our control. While the region is mostly inhabited by Russians, Ukranians and Tatars, there is a small population of Crimean Germans in the regions and a history of Gothic settlement in the region that led some of our advisors to urge us reconsider our position in the region. What most of our advisors agree on is that we are to prop a Tatar government in the region, which would undoubtedly endear us to the Turk and Muslim population which favor them. To go further than that, others suggest we further take control over the Port of Sevastopol or even that we exercise heavy oversight over the new government, turning it into a de facto colonial government.

When we pushed to create a unified Scandinavia, we believed it would be always under our supervision, but recently we are starting to doubt that. Despite stil being loyal to us and acting on German interests, Scandinavia is slowly becoming more and more proeminent in the world stage, worrying us that they will eventually break free from us and become a great power on their own. While they would undoubtedly be hostile towards Russia, the Danish claims on Schleswig would be an excuse for them to turn on us as well, that is why our advisors have suggested we "defang" the Lion of the North by separating Denmark from it, assuring they can't use its claims against us and leaving a buffer between us and this possible new great power.

The Russian Withdrawal from the Caucasus was a turbulent one. The Russian officials fleed hastly from the country, with some Cossacks and sympathetic Georgians, Armenians and Ossetians putting some resistance, but that has since been taken care of. \n\nNow, with the Southern Caucasus united under $COUNTRY$$, our uneasy federation has to face the question of who will lead it. Of course, such decision is bound to anger whichever major ethnicity isn't chosen to lead or nation.
Unite Southern Cossack Hosts

§RGain 7.5 infamy§! While we unite most of the Cossack groups in the region—namely the Azov, Black Sea, Don and Kuban Hosts—we are yet to unite all of them, with the Astrakhan and Terek Cossacks yet to be liberated from foreign rule. We could use our newfound freedom to proclaim our ambitions to unite with our Cossack brethen!
Lacks Writing System
Reunite Lombardy and Venetia

§RGain 5 infamy§! Despite being our traditional enemies, we have found ourselves in a brief union with Venice under the Austrian Empire, in the form of the Kingdom of Lombardy-Venice. While the kingdom became defunct with the Austrian loss of Milan, we are now our own nation and reclaiming the union between Lombardy and Venice could not only help us secure you position in the world stage but also signal that we are willing to put old rivalries aside for Italy's future.

With our hegemony over Italy secured, our new allies in Lombardy have petitioned us to back their claim to Venice. Both Milan and Venice where united under the Kingdom of Lombardy-Venice within the Austrian Empire. Sadly, we couldn't secure hold over both Venice and Milan, meaning the kingdom was effectively thorn apart. Now, the warhawks in Milan ask us to placate their nationalistic sentiments with military action against Austria for the liberation of Venice.
Lombardy-Venice
Lombardy-Venice
Lombardy-Venice

The Shogunate exercised direct control over the domain of the Matsumae clans from 1799 to 1821. Despite their position being pivotal for the defense of Japan against foreign powers, the Matsumae Clan has long been seen with a certain degree of distrust, with many shady dealings with the local Ainu and constantly getting in trouble with local merchants. \n\nNow we once again move to exert direct control over their domains, with the Matsumae Clan being given a high position in the position of Roju and being moved to Edo for the time being.

Takahiro Matsumae—the new Lord of the Matsumae Clan—has finally come of age! The Matsumae have been relegated to a significant council position in Edo ever since we took over their domain in 1834. With his coming of age, the Shogun has once again granted the Matsumae Clan control over their domains in Ezo, tasking the young lord to build a new castle in the island.

Despite the Matsumae Clan financial troubles, the clan managed to—with the help of some smart budgeting and popular funding—build the new Fukuyama Castle in the south of Ezo. Sadly, with the opening of our ports to foreigners, we have to once again take control over most of the Matsumae Clan's domains, with Takahiro Matsumae being relegated to being a magistrate of the Shogun for the forseeable future.
Reclaim the Legacy of Inal the Great

§RGain 2.5 infamy§! Ever since the construction of the fortress of Mozdok in 1764, the Russian Empire began to threaten the independence of the Circassian tribes, provoking our resistance in what became known as the Russo-Circassian War. With the fall of the Grand Principality of Kabardia recently, the only remaining independent polity in the region became our proud confederation, with the Russian Empire having pushed as far as the Kuban river. Despite this dire position, we once were united under a great leader—Inal the Great—being known as the Grand Principality of Circassia. If we were able to push back the Russians and assert our independence, the world would have no choice but to recognize our claims to our ancient lands and our status in the world stage.
Caucasus Imamate

Imam Ghazi Muhammad was once just a teacher, growing to popularity, promoting the sharia law in the region and proposing passive resistance to the Russians in the region. But, by late 1829 the renewed Russian expansion in the region made him change his instance, with the creation of a military-theocratic state—the Caucasian Imamate. His rule had a series of successes and drawbacks, with him eventually being forced to retreat to his hometown and base of power, the town of Gimry. As the Russian punitive expedition followed him into the region, having a hard time approaching the Imam's hiding place—a fortified home where Ghazi Muhammad and other 13 other defenders made a last defense against the Russian troops. Knowing they wouldn't be able to defend the building for long, the Imam jumped out of the home with a sword, with other defenders following him. Only two defenders were able to survive the onslaught, being able to flee from the Russian forces. With Ghazi Muhammad dead—despite a brief power struggle—Hamzat Bek was elected as the new Imam.

Imam Hamzat Bek's rule was different than that of his predecessor. Focusing on consolidating the Imamate and the Sharia in the region. Sadly, that wouldn't mean his rule would last. One of his campaigns against the Khanums of Khuzakh would end up in a bloody massacre, leading to a blood feud with the remaining relatives of the Avar ruling family. Imam Hamzat Bek would be assassinated by Hajji Uthman and Hajji Murad, with Uthman being killed by the Imam's guards.

Following the assassination of Hazmat Bek, another election would be held to decide the new Imam, with one of the survivors of the Battle of Gimry—Shamil—being elected, soon being responsible for rekindling the conflicts against the Russians. \n\nEnjoying considerable popularity in the Imamate and having narrowly lost to Hamzat Bek in the previous election, Shamil was many scholar's first pick for the new Imam of Dagestan. Despite that, Shamil still faced fierce opposition from Tashaw-Hadji who—upon losing to Shamil by one vote—would retire to Chechnya, where he would becoming Imam, dividing the Imamate in two, refusing to recognize Shamil's authority, despite being willing to join ally with him against Russian incursions.
Meet Chechen Leaders

Despite being under the grasp of Gazhi Muhammad and Hamzat Bek, the Chechens broke off from the Dagestani Imamate with the election of Imam Shamil, with his opponent becoming in the election the Imam of Chechnya, refusing to recognize Shamil's Authority. With the developments in the conflict against Russia, it is clear that we have no choice but to secure the Chechen-Dagestani trade routes and unite the two Imamates. With this in mind, Imam Shamil has sent a letter to the most proeminent Chechen leader besides Tashaw-Hadji, proposing a meeting with the many Chechen leaders, with the intention of being elected by them as the Imam of Chechnya.

As Russia prepares to disarm the Chechens and turn us into serfs, Imam Shamil of Dagestan is trying to convince our greatest military leader—Isa of Ghendargen—to support him as the sole military and spiritual leader of both Dagestan and Chechnya. After much deliberation, the Chechen leaders held a meeting in Urus-Martan to consider Shamil's offer.
The Chechens have accepted our offer, investing Imam Shamil as the sole military and spiritual leader of Chechnya, once again unifying us in our struggle against Russia!
Being unwilling to bow to Shamil the council of Chechen military leaders ultimately decided to reject Shamil's offer, prefering to fare alone against the might of Russia!

Amidst our current conflict with Russia, the leadership of the Chechens is divided between a plethora of military leaders—the strongest of which is Isa of Ghendargen—and the Imam of Chechnya—Tashaw-Hadji. While the Chechens can have different spiritual and military leaders, we have to decide who is ultimately in charge.

As our conflicts with Russia intensify, the Russian Emperor Nicholas I came to the Caucasus for a visit. While we don't plan on attacking the Tsar directly, it seems that the military forces in the region were ordered to extend the olive branch for Shamil and his men. 

Today, Shamil and his men met with a small Russian force led by general Kluki von Klugenau. The two leaders would sit try to negotiate, as von Klugenau would try to convince Shamil to lay down his weapons and come with him for an audience with the Emperor with a plea for pardon. 

This request hardly should be taken into considering, seeing that Shamil would be surrendering Dagestan to Russian hands if he were to accept.

Following our conquest of the Caucasus—which is still ongoing—Emperor Nicholas I scheduled a visit to the region, being accompanied by a military delegation and visiting key cities in the region. The Russian command—led by General Kluki von Klugenau—was ordered to attempt to negotiate with Imam Shamil, the so-called Imam of Dagestan, which is in open conflict with our empire since his ascension to power in 1834. Surprisingly, it seems that von Klugenau's mission was a success, with the general bringing Shamil to the Emperor, on the occasion Shamil would plead for a pardon, which the Emperor would concede. Shamil would be sent to St. Petersburg, having essentially handed Dagestan to us.
Avenge the Torghut Migration

§RGain 5 infamy§! Despite willingly joining the Russian Empire upon our arrival in the region, the Russians did nothing but spit on our loyalty and force a great portion of our people to flee back to our homeland with the other Oirat tribes. We have long been an asset to the Russian army, our men bled and died for the Russian Tsars, only for them to encroach in our territory and limit our autonomy. Eventually, our previous khans were forced to organize a migration back to our homeland, with the Russians trying to stop our return to Dzungaria, turning the migration into a costly endeavor. Now, while we are just a shadow of an once mighty khanate, if we want to restore our status a regional player we must declare our intentions to avenge the Torghut Migration, and retake the territories that the Russian Tsars unlawfully took from us!
Reform the Circassian Confederation

On the wake of the Russian threat and the fall of Eastern Circassia—also known as Kabardia—the tribes of Western Circassia were forced to form a firm confederation, with manjis from 12 tribes voting on a president to lead the defense against the Russian Empire. We came a long way from the days of scrambling to muster a cohesive defense against the Russians, but after almost a century of conflict, we have not only driven the Russians from our ancient territories but also managed to modernize our state and diplomacy, finally being recognized as a proper nation by the great powers of powers. It is finaly time we leave our confederation behind and decide what kind of country we want Circassia to be in the forseeable future.

Molded by its struggles against the Russian Empire, Circassia is now unrecognizeable from its state at the turn of the century. Once a confederation of loose tribes, we have properly reformed into a sturdy federation, ready to chart history as its own nation, having asserted its independence from Russia. \n\nNow, all that is left is for us to decide how this new federation will present itself.

In 1870, after a trip to Jamaica, the schooner—under Captain Lorenzo Dow Baker—brought the banana fruit to the United States for the first time. The tropical fruit would grow to become quite popular, being a nutritious and cheap alternative to locally grown fruit, with a dozen bananas costing around 0.25 cents, which would translate to only two apples.

Following the introduction of the banana fruit to the United States, the fruit came directly into contact with out interests in tropical countries. Paired with the construction of railways lines in Costa Rica, a American bussinessman—Henry Meiggs—would start to plant bananas in the country to feed his workers. Meiggs would be assisted by his nephew—Minor Cooper Keith—who would assume the enterprise after Meiggs' death. Keith would go on to create the Tropical Trading and Transport Company, the first company to operate cheap tropical fruit plantations in Central American goverments, with huge grants from the local goverment, starting the era of Banana-driven Politics dominating our sphere of influence in Central America.
Central American Fruit Corporations

Staring with the foundation of Tropical Trading and Transport Company and the Boston Fruit Company—which would later merge into the United Fruit Company—American multinational fruit companies would spread across Central America, taking advantage of cheap local labor to profit, exerting significant influence over the local governments and, of course, granting us cheap fruit to feed our people.
Banana Republic

Our economy has been essentially hijacked by American multinational corporations, with them dominanting our country with huge plantations and infrastructure projects to support their exporting of tropical fruit. They exert a great deal of influence in our goverment and making it hard for the government to do anything but grant them further concessions.

With the growing power of American fruit companies in our nation, they guaranteed a great deal of influence in our politics, leading to us becoming essentially a "Banana Republic", as our economy is steered toward serving these companies' plantations.

Our new government has posed a significant pushback to the interest of the American economies that previously dominated our economy. We are finally free to act against these companies, nationalizing their investments and once again taking control of our economy.

Circassia has long been graced with the help of British adventurers, usually bringing weapons for us to fight or even joining our fight against Russia. Recently, the ship of one of these adventurers—James Stanislaus Bell—was detained by the Russian navy, enraging the British parliament, rising tensions between the two nations. While the tensions between the two nations are bound to subside, adventurers like Bell have played a crucial role in not only popularizing the Circassian struggle, but also training our army and helping in our fight against Russia.

Poland has been restless ever since the Congress of Vienna. While there was some relief in the liberties we got from the pressure of foreign powers such as Britain, it was clear that Russian overlordship meant that these liberties had their days numbered. As the Russian Tsars slowly took away our liberty, we have finally decided that enough was enough, with an uprising sparked by a group of young Polish officers led by Piotr Wysocki. After a brief struggle and the fleeing of Grand Duke Constantine—brother of the Russian Tsar Nicholas I—the revolutionaries took control of the city, with the new Polish National Government declared after a brief dictatorship by Józef Grzegorz Chlopicki. The government has elected Prince Adam Jerzy Czartoryski, and Poland now prepares for the Russian retaliation to the November Uprising.

Despite facing the raging ire of the Russian Bear, Poland—against all odds—managed to inflict a blowing defeat to the Russian pride, with Russia finally accepting to recognize the independence of the Kingdom of Poland. While we are far from our former glory, we have proved to the powers of Europe that Poland is not yet lost! To celebrate our victory, the Sejm has declared the end of the Polish National Government, restoring the 1815 Constitution of the Kingdom of Poland. The only matter left to decide is the question if Poland is to remain a kingdom or not, with some of our more radical advisors favoring the republican model instead.

Our time is up.

Despite our attempts to compromise with the Tsar, the Russian Bear isn't interested in compromising. As his plan fails, Chlopicki resigns. A new government is formed and the Russian forces under Field Marshal Hans Karl von Diebitsch cross into Poland. 

It is all or nothing.
Polish National Government

Despite the exhausted and half-trained nature of the Hellenic Army, the refusal of the London Protocol by Kapodistrias managed to squeeze a little more of steam from the Hellenic people, allowing us to defeat the Ottoman Empire and get our desired border, with Greece not only stretching from the Gulf of Ambracian Gulf to Mount Ossa, but also including Crete and the Aegean Islands. \n\nKapodistrias may not have lived to see our victory, but his actions granted Greece not only its independence, but also its dignity.

The Murder of Ioannis Kapodistrias put a severe strain in our society, with his brother—Augustinos Kapodistrias—leaving the country in anarchy and removing any hope of beating the Ottomans and pushing for the border following the Ambracian Gulf-Ossa Line that the late Ioannis envisioned for us. Luckily, we still managed to assert our independence from the Ottomans, securing a border along the Arta–Volos line, while being able to retain Greece. While not ideal, this "revised" London Protocol certainly put us in a better position than before, even if we have to deal with the chaos left by Augustinos Kapodistrias' government...

Suffering from the same failings as his brothers but having none of his virtues, the government of Augustinos Kapodistrias was bound to fail sooner rather than later. With the country being throw more and more into anarchy, Augustinos ends his 6-month long rule by resigning, leaving Greece to be government by the Administrative Council for the time being.

With Greece being thrown into anarchy, the Administrative Council has already gone through 5 different governments, with the Hellenic State hardly being the state our people fought so hard to establish. The situation became so dire that the foreign powers once again came to interfere into Greek affairs, suggesting the Catholic Prince Otto Friedrich Ludwig von Bayern of Bavaria as a king to restore order. While the idea of a Catholic German as our king would be unacceptable a few months ago, with the chaos that has taken Greece by storm, this seems like the only option out of this mess.

The Sarhad Highlands were conquered by Kalat under the rule of Mir Nasir Khan, the Great. Despite Kalat's authority since the death of Mir Nasir Khan being only nominal the recent collapse of the khanate that followed the British invasion, threw the highlands into anarchy, with the formal authority of Kalat ceasing to unify the region and leading to it officially being divided by various chiefdoms around settlements such as Bampur, Dozdab and Bashkard.

Being born as a confederation of tribes against Mughal expansion, the Brahui Confederacy—now known of as the Khanate of Kalat—rose as the first unified polity in Balochistan, quickly subjulgating neighboring tribes, breaking off from Mughal suzerainty in 1666 under Mir Ahmad Khan I, and from that point on being able to butt heads with local Mughal governors to preserve its independence and acquire more territory. \n\nThe Khanate of Kalat reached its peak under Mir Nasir Khan, leaving chiefdoms such as Bampur, Makran, Kharan and Las Belas under our overlordship. While nominally our vassals, ever since the death of Mir Nasir Khan, our authority over them became almost non-existent, with our subjects looking for any excuse to break away from our rule even if we already can't really exercise our authority in practice.
Put an End to Kharani Treachery

While their allegiance bounced between many polities over the years, the oasis town of Kharan came under the authority of the Khanate of Kalat during the rule of Mir Nasir Khan I. Nowdays, with Mir Nasir Khan I long gone, our control over the chiefs of Kharan grows more and more fragile, with its proximity to Kandahar and Kabul posing a risk to our overlordship over the city. Negleting to assert our authority over Kharan could mean losing the city to our northern neighbors, showing our weaknesses to the our enemies in the North.
End Jamote Autonomy

The Jamote Clan came to rule Las Bela ever since Mahabat Khan supported descendents of Qurayshite Abdul Manauf claim the region. Ever since then, Las Bela has been our tributary, paying us half of their taxes and being closely related to the Khans of Kalat, going go far as their current ruler being the son-in-law of our khan. Las Bela grew quite prosperous under our protection and our ending their autonomy would allow us to directly tax their wealthy Hindu merchant families, greatly helping our coffers.
End Makrani Autonomy

Makran came under our rule during the reign of Mir Nasir Khan I, with the local chiefs currently being somewhat loyal to us, at least inland. In reality, Makran is divided between the inland chiefdoms and the coast, with our authority over the interior being somewhat solid, especially in comparison to the costal settlements, which since them have been switching allegiances back and forth between us and Muscat, mostly falling Muscati influence these days. Back in the days of Mir Nasir Khan I, we held control over the important city Gwadar, but the port was gifted to future sultan of Oman, which would eventually start to betray our kindness by swaying the costal cities to be under his domain. If we were to put an end to the autonomy of the Makrani chiefs in the interior, we surely could also reclaim our old costal holdings as well, once again taking control of Gwadar.
Cement our Rule over the Sarhad Highlands

While the Sarhad Highlands were subjulgated by the great Mir Nasir Khan I, after his death, our authority over the region quickly deteriorated, with cities such as Bampur and Dozdab quickly falling under nominal vassalage, barely even paying taxes to us. If we don't assert out rule over the region quickily, being so far away from our core lands in Kalat proper, should our Iranian neighbors turn their eyes towards our land, we would be powerless to stop the Shahs of Iran from taking Bampur from us.

Despite declining with quite a bit with the death of Mir Nasir Khan I, we managed to still assert our superiority against the other Baluchi clans and chiefdoms, allowing us to end their autonomy and assert ourselves as the sole authority in Baluchistan. The Brahui clans now sit as the most proeminent power in Baluchistan, allowing us to celebrate our victory against not only the other clans, but also against the foreign powers that once sought to topple us and exploit the Baluchi clans!

With our takeover of the lands of the Jamote Clan, we are now in full control of their old center of power—the city of Bela. Under Jamote rule, Bela grew quite prosperous, gathering a community of Hindu merchants that now lies under our rule. As our governors set in Bela, the city and its merchants grow uneasy, unsure if the Khan's new administration will rock their bussiness. Seeing to that, they—backed with the new local governor—have petitioned the Khan to implement some small reforms that would greatly help those merchants continue to make bussiness within the city of Bela and also spreading through the rest of our domains.
The Legacy of Mir Nasir Khan, the Great

§RGain 5 infamy§! Mir Nasir Khan I of Kalat was the greatest leader of Kalat and the Baluchi people in general. His domain were the highest point in Baluchi history, assuring the superiority of the Brahui clans for quite a while. Now that we have modernized and centralized our realm, it is time to recover everything we lost since the days of Mir Nasir Khan, establishing our rule from Bandar-e Abbas to Karachi.

Our authority on the coastal settlements of Makran was always loose at best. That is why, when the future sultan of Oman Said Taimur was exiled from Muscat, we were more than happy to grand him the port of Gwadar. Ever since then, we only lost more and more control over our coast, with the Omanis that we once welcomed with open arms seizing the opportunity to control the costal settlements of Makran, while we were barely being able to exercise our authority inland. But now, with the reconquest of Gwadar we finally righted this wrong. While we usually wouldn't be very interested in controling a costal settlement, the Omanis have developed the place quite a built, even building a fort that would help us better exercise our authority in the region.

While we came close to reconquering Greece with the arrival of Ibrahim Pasha, the Eyalet of Morea was essentially extinct ever since the Greek rebels took control of the peninsula. Now, despite the foreign pressure to accept Greek liberty, we have finally put an end to the Greek revolt and, while most of Greece still is agitated against our rule, with our occupation, it is clear that this will eventually die down...
Save the Circassians

§RGain 5 infamy§!While we never subjulgated Circassia entirely, we exercised some authority in the region for a while, until the Russians made us drop all of our claims in 1828. Over the years, as Russian agression grew more and more, the people of Circassia have relied more and more on us—and the British—to help them against the Russian Bear. Now that we have proved that we can make the bear bleed, it is the perfect time to save Circassian from its claws, liberating the Circassian highlanders and ensuring their eternal loyalty against the Russians.

With our take over of the Circassian homeland, we see the region completely changed by its century-long struggle against Russia, with many Circassians having fled the region to escape the Russian ire, with those that remain being completely battered from the years of fight against Russia. With the news of our victory spreading across our empire, many of the Circassians that sought refuge in our land seek to return to their homeland, ready to be our loyal subjects. Despite that, many of our advisors still debate what to do with the region, with many suggesting we grant them self-rule under our empire, while some—more chavunistic elements in our ranks—suggest we leave it clear that we are their new overlords and that things aren't getting any better than under Russia...

While we have long enjoyed some degree of autonomy, the tyrants of $FROMCOUNTRY$ came knocking, eager to end our liberty. While we aren't totally powerless to fight them, maybe uniting with $FROMCOUNTRY$ would be better for us and our people.

The Danubian Principalities of Wallachia and Moldavia have been occupied by our troops since the war of 1828-29. The populace is becoming increasingly resentful of this, especially as we have confiscated Wallachia and Moldavia's treasuries for the time being. As a result of the growing unrest, we formed two commission to work on the Regulamentul Organic of both states, to shape their government, allowing us to end our occupation while maintaining our influence on both principalities. With both commissions having sent their finished drafts to us, the Regulamentul Organic will the basis of government of the principalities, while assuring their autonomy within the Ottoman Empire and their status as our protectorates, allowing us to intervene in the region if needed. Being adopted by Moldavia on 13 July 1831 and now by Wallachia, we have finally restored the civilian governments of the principalities, letting the Ottoman Sultan appoint new voivods.

The Danubian Principalities of Wallachia and Moldavia have been occupied by Russian troops since the war of 1828-29. Luckily the Russians had no intention of occupying the Principalities indefinitely, with the Bear working to establish a new organic law that would allow the Danubian Principalities to restore their civil government while still being our vassal under Russian influence. Now, this new organic law is finished, with both Moldavia and Wallachia adopting the Regulamentul Organic as a constitutional base, allowing us to once again appoint voivods to the region and ending the Russian occupation.

Following the Moldavian Revolution, we had sent our advisors to the Principality to try to defuse the situation. Now, with the situation worsening, we are forced to move troops into the region, occupying Moldavia until the liberal agitation in the Danubian Principalities calms down.

Following the Wallachian Revolution, the revolutionaries in the Principality we have sent a request to the Ottoman Empire to put down the revolutionaries before it was too late. Instead, it seems that the revolutionaries have successfully endeared the Ottoman Sultan to their cause, leading to them denouncing our occupation of Moldavia. It seems that we have no option but to move to occupy Wallachia as well.

It seems that renouncing the Regulamentul Organic worked in our favor, with the act endearing the Ottomans to our cause! The Ottomans have expressed support in our cause, denouncing Russia for their occupation of Wallachia and warning us that we must prepare for war!

After a small campaign against the Ottomans, we have asserted our position in the Danubian Principalities, showing the Ottomans who really has the power over Wallachia and Moldavia. \n\nWith the Ottoman Empire leaving it clear that it can't be trusted with overlordship of the region, we have decided to establish a more permanent position in the region, with the only other power with a say in the region—Austria—being too weak and in our debt, unable to act against our subjulgation of the Danubian Principalities.

After a prolonged campaign against Russia, we have asserted our position in the Danubian Principalities,  showing the Russians who really has the power over Wallachia and Moldavia. \n\nNot only have we revoked the Russian protectorate over Wallachia and Moldavia, we have also reconquered some of our loses from war in 1829, asserting our position in the region and restoring some of our prestige.

With the Wallachian Revolution being put down and the signining of the Convention of Balta Liman, it is about time we once again leave Moldavia. \n\nWhile some concessions were made to the Ottoman Empire, it only means that now they have a bigger stake in preserving our joint rule over the principalities.

Ever since the Von Rennenkampf's expedition to South Ossetia, the Ossetians on our side of the Caucasus have been weary of a possible expedition to the north. The North Ossetians tried to reach out to us leading to our expedition to Ingushetia, trying to surrender to us and avoid a military expedition to their territory. Sadly, while we were not keen on accepting their surrender in the first place, their preparations for a possible expedition left us no choice but to dispatch an expedition against them. The expedition was led by Major-General Ivan Nikolayevich Abkhazov—a Georgian noble highly regarded by Paskevich—with his men advancing into Suargom Pass, where his troops met the most resistance, engaging in a 3-day long battle, with the villages that offered resistance after that being burned to the ground. Some further expeditions would be needed after Abkhazov's initial campaign, but Ossetia was essentially secured by the Major-General's ruthless actions.

The Ossetians have long been a nuisance to our administration. Not only do they pose a significant threat to the Georgian Military Road—our main connection to our overlords in St. Petersburg—but they conduct significant raids in our territory, drawing the anger of the Georgian Nobility that urges us to deal with those bandits. Luckily, the General Ivan Fyodorovich Paskevich—Count of Erivan and High Comissioner of the Caucasus—obtained permission from Tsar Nicholas I to act on the Ossetians, ordering an expedition to be headed by Second Lieutenant Paul Andreas von Rennenkampff. Von Rennenkampff's expedition would be swift, with his troops moving from village to village, but eventually finding significant resistance from the locals. After a heroic last stand from the locals near the village of Kola, Von Rennenkampf reached an agreement with the remaining Ossetians, with the region being pacified after the Ossetians were spared from serfdom by Paskevich.

As Russia prepares to disarm the Chechens and turn us into serfs, Imam Shamil of Dagestan—after being defeated and routed from his own lands—is trying to convince our greatest military leader—Isa of Ghendargen—to support him as the sole military and spiritual leader of both Dagestan and Chechnya. After much deliberation, the Chechen leaders held a meeting in Urus-Martan to consider Shamil's offer.
The Naibs of Imam Shamil

§RThis will lead to war with Russia! Gain 7.5 infamy§! During his rule, Imam Shamil—as the spiritual and military leader of Dagestan and Chechnya—reformed the administration of the region by appointing naibs—regional governors to oversee recruitment, taxes and the Sharia Law. While most of these naibs acted within the Imam's territory—such as Muhammad Amin—acting outside of his territory, with some naibs conducting raids in Georgia and trying to stir up resistance in Ossetia, to no avail...

While the Circassians nominally follow Islamic teachings, the people of the North Caucasus in general historically had hard time trully adhering to any foreign religion. Such, some ambassadors from the Abzakh clan have asked Shamil—the Imam of Dagestan and Chechnya—to send a naib to not only properly introduce Islam to Circassia, but also unite the two nations in their struggle against Russia. \n\nWhat came from it was the arrival of the warrior and scholar Muhammad-Amin in Circassia, uniting most Circassian clans under him. While helpful, his total control over Circassia would mean becoming a puppet of the Imanate. Luckily, the clans of Shapsugh and Abkhaz still resist his rule, assuring us some degree of autonomy, at least until we can have a parliament deciding the fate of Circassia...

After being a signifcant thorn in our side, Imam Shamil struck us once again! While he was once restricted to acting in Chechnya and Dagestan, now his influence has spread widely along the Caucasus, with his naibs—Muhammad Amin and Daniyal Bek—establishing control over Circassia and Elisu respectivelly, effectively invading our territory. If we don't act now we risk letting his agents further penetrate our holdings in the Caucasus...

Mir-Fatah-Agha—also known as Mushthaid—was the head of the Caucasian Ulema ever since the Treaty of Turkmenchay. He was invited by the High Comissioner of the Caucasus, Ivan Paskevich, to be advise him and help him reign in the Muslim population. Being a Shiite, he naturally succeeded greatly in quelling any unrest within the Shia population, even bolstering recruitment among them for the Russian Army. Sadly, with the departure of Ivan Paskevich to Poland, the following administration didn't make things easy on Mushthaid, with him being eventually forced to return to Persia in 1841, leaving no strong religious figure in the Caucasus besides Imam Shamil of Dagestan.
Our Aliies Across the Mountains

§RGain 5 infamy§! While our influence is restricted mostly to the Sunni peoples of the North Caucasus, we manages to get some contacts in the other side of the mountains, with figures such as Daniyal Bek and the neighboring Dagestani tribes being devoted allies to our cause. Of course, crossing the mountains itself would be rather difficult to do, but we could—instead try to attack the Russians around Baku, creating a corridor between Elisu and our holdings, while also depriving Russia of one of its most important routes to secure the Caucasus...

Widespread Russophilia been developing in Austrian Ruthenia for a long while and, ever since Pan-Slavist Intellectual Mikhail Pogodin visited Lemberg in 1835 and 1839, the city's intelligentsia started to drift more and more into Russophilia, with this sentiment only growing stronger with the Austrians leaving the region under the control of the local Polish nobility after the 1848 Revolutions. Figures like Denys Zubrytsky made so that, now—despite some hurdles caused by the war and our occupation of the region—the people from Ruthenia have formally pledged to join Russia as the Fourth Russian nation, joing Great Russia, Little Russia and White Russia as Red Russia.

Despite not being part of our initial ambitions, we have successfully expelled the Turks from our borders, leading to a big celebration in Bucharest! What many naive revolutionaries fail to undestand it that, sadly, Moldavia is still under Russian occupation and, having coaxed the Ottoman Empire into surpressingour revolution, it is clear that Russia won't let us remain free for long...

While we trusted that our allies in Constantinople would be able to supressed the Wallachian Revolution, we have been throughly disappointed by their effort, with the revolutionaries in Bucharest celebrating their victory at this very instant. What they fail to understand is that their victory is to be short-lived, with our empire not being willing to risk this revolution reigniting the fires of occupied Moldavia, forcing us to act fast or risk having a bigger problem in our hands.

Despite not being the first permanent public theatre in Iasi—that title goes to the Théâtre des Variétés, founded in 1831—the Great Theatre of Moldavia was the first national theatre in all of Romania, founded when the Romanian language troupe, led by Costache Caragiali, was united with the French troupe, under a single direction of Vasile Alecsandri, all under the management of Costache Caragiali. \n\nThe Theatre would soon become one of the most prestigious theatrical institutions in all of Romania, shadowing ever similar institutions in Wallachia.

While the Commonwealth didn't exist as a state for more than a generation, it is still a synonym for the Polish state, with a majority of the Sejm seeing our state not as a ressurgent Polish state, but as a continuation of the Commonwealth that was under an illegal occupation for the last 30 or so years. \n\nWhile this is a great point of pride for our nation, with our recent victory against Russia, the current realities have hit us and we have to face the fact that we are even weaker than the Commonwealth during its 3rd Partition, with our enemies having grown stronger since then. With our neighbors eyeing up our every movement—waiting for us to provoke them—maybe we should let the fires of the Commonwealth die, so Poland can live in peace for another day.

By itself, the Polish victory against the Russian Empire following the November Uprising was a surprise by itself. While we feared that it would possibly spill over into our Polish-speaking lands, we would never have imagined the uprising would actually succeed. Now, to our shock, the revived Polish has claimed to be a direct continuation of the Commonwealth, freeing itself from more than 30 years of occupation. \n\nNonetheless, we shouldn't let this slide! As the news reach us, many in our cabinet now are suggesting we take action against Poland while they are still exhausted from their war with Russia, allowing us to remeind the Polish what happened to their precious Commonwealth last time!

We have succeed in subduing Poland once again! Now, with our allies meeting with the defeated Polish leadership and our allies in Warsaw, it is about time we decide what to do with this excuse of a state, to return Poland to the dustbin of of history!

Despite our failure in subduing Poland during the 1831 November Uprising, it seems the Polish government in Warsaw was so emboldened by their victory that they decided to invite both Austria and Prussia into war against them, assuring their sweetdefeat, with $FROMCOUNTRY$ being reasonable enough to return Poland to us after their victory. While it is humiliating to have the Germans deal with Poland in our place, at least we are back in control of Poland.

After our campaign against the newly-independent Polish state, $FROMCOUNTRY$ has suggested that—seeing as Russia was unable to keep the Polish scum in check—it is only right that we partition it once again between ourselves. We are to be given any territory that we gained during the 3rd Partition of Poland, plus any territory that Poland got directly eastwards of it, with the rest to be given to our ally.

In the wake of the liberal revolutions that spread across Europe, the Papal States is caught right in the middle—literally—of the nationalist agitation over Italian Unification. With the Pope having long played a central role in Italian politics, the Prime-Minister of the Kingdom of Sardinia-Piedmont—Vincenzo Giobertipublished a series of works defending the unification of Italy in the form of a confederation under Papal leadership. While Pope Pius IX is somewhat open to the idea of political reforms, the dominance of Anti-clerical and Freemasonry groups over the movement for Italian unification makes it somewhat dangerous for the Pope to show support to such a hostile cause.
In the Name of God, For Our Freedom and Yours

§RGain 12.5 infamy This decision will disappear after 1848! This will enable you to be called to intervene in the liberal revolutions happening around you—namely Hungary, Bohemia and Poznan!§! Coined during the November Uprising, the motto "For Our Freedom and Yours" would become widely popular among the Polish revolutionaries, signaling to the world that freedom for Poland would mean freedom for other peoples enslaved by Russia. The motto would allign with the ideas of Prince Adam Jerzy Czartoryski, who believed Russia had cultivated slaves instead of allies—an error which costed Russia the Polish lands. While we haven't achieved our objective of freeing all of the slaves of the Tsarist regime, Czartoryski—worried about Poland's place in Europe—has been reaching out to neighboring liberal and nationalist movements, suggesting that Poland could directly support future insurrections against the opressive powers of Europe, granting them the fire(power) they need to secure their freedom and—in the process—ours too.

Ever since the end of the November Uprising, we have slowly built a network beyond our borders, establishing contact with Polish aristocrats and revolutionaries in Austria and Prussia. Recently, those contacts in Poznan, Krakow and Lviv have informed us that they were planning on revolting simultaneously on 21 February 1846, to take both Prussia and Austria by surprise. Naturally, they seek our support in their endeavor, to once again reunite with our nation. While we could allow them to attempt their uprising in 1846, it may be better to order them to prepare the ground until we invade either Prussia or Austria, to let me stun the German powers with the defection of the Polish subjects.

As we made an effort to coordinate the efforts of the Polish elites and revolutionaries abroad, now that we cross into enemy territory we are greated by the local Polish population as liberators, with our allies in their lands quickily taking up arms and declaring their allegiance to our state. With their support, we are sure to destroy our enemies in the field of battle!

It would seem that the backwardness of our state has painted our efforts in a bad light for the Galician peasants. While some members of the local Polish nobility and intelligentsia tried to stir up a revolt in the Austrian Crown of Galicia and Lodomeria, the prospect of fighting the Austrian Empire to join another backwards state which enforced serfdom wasn't taken very well by the peasantry in Galicia. They have—with some support of local Austrian officials—taken to slaughtering a good portion of the revolting szlachta, effectively killing the revolt before it could start.

While we always feared that our Polish minority could cause us problems in face of a resurgent Polish state, we could never imagine that our enemies would penetrate so deeply into our holdings that they would defect on a moments notice. As we wage war on the Poles, a considerable portion of the Polish people in our territory have betrayed us and sworn loyalty to our enemies.

While we held great interest in the Slavic Congress being held in Prague, we, sadly, our representatives weren't allowed to enter Prague, forcing us to contend with having our Galician allies speak on our behalf. Sadly, the Congress—after going on for 10 days—was cut short by hostilities breaking out between the Austrian Army and radical students. As Prague is put in a state of siege, most of the Congress attendants left Prague, either in disgusts or in fear of being persecuted. Such an act of barbarism hardly surprises it, but—seeing the chaos endured by the Austrian Empire—it may be the perfect time to rattle our cage and attack the Austrian Empire in their moment of weakness.

With liberal revolutions spreading across Prussia and all of the German Confederation, the King of Prussia has granted a pardon to all of the Polish revolutionaries arrested by his government. This, of course, has stirred a lot trouble around Poznan and the rest of their Polish lands, with demonstrations leading to the formation of Polish militias. Prince Adam Czartoryski tried to go to Berlin to negotiate, in a fruitless attempt to disarm the situation, but now, as the militias are desarmed, the Prussian soldiers advancing into the region began to violently crack down on the Polish population leading to the peasantry rising up against their Prussian overlords. Naturally, while this led to the end of talks with the Prussian governments, our allies in Poznan have formally asked for us to assist them in liberating Greater Poland, letting them finally join our state!

As liberal agitation spread into Europe, revolutionaries in Hungary have formally taken control over once Austrian-dominated kingdom, with the revolutionary Lajos Kossuth at the head of the Hungarian National Defense Commission, currently ruling the precarious country. The "Hungarian Cause" has long interested the more revolutionary-inclined elements in our government, with many in our current cabinet seeing Hungary as a brotherly nation in the struggle against foreign powers. At this point, we are under heavy pressure from the Sejm and our own cabinet to rally behind Hungary and help them liberate themselves from Habsburg rule!

While we always suspicious of how the newly-independent Polish state could threaten our domains, we could never imagine that they would be so bold as to openly support our rebellious subjects at the first opportunity. Now, with revolts spreading in our territory, the Polish have officially shown support to our enemies, leaving it clear that they would be willing to declare war on us to segure the liberal revolutions that spread across our country!

As we made an effort to coordinate the efforts of the Polish elites and revolutionaries abroad, now that we cross into enemy territory we are greated by the local Polish population as liberators, with our allies in their lands quickily taking up arms and declaring their allegiance to our state. With their support, we are sure to destroy our enemies in the field of battle!
The Prussian Threat

§RGain 5 infamy§! While we already have an interest in parts of East Prussia—such as Masuria—due to their Polish minority, the most important part of the region—Königsberg—has long been out of our ambitions, with it—along with most of the Prussian province of East Prussia—having been part of Ducal Prussia, with the region being a fiefdom of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth for over 100 years. \n\nMore recently, Prince Adam Jerzy Czartoryski—while withdrawn from politics after his father's death—published his book "Essay on Diplomacy", where the former Foreign Minister of Tsar Alexander I would urge Russia to access the Prussian threat and incorporate East Prussia into a ressurgent Poland. While these ideas wouldn't come to fruition with the November Uprising breaking later in that year, the Prussian threat that once loomed over Russia now looms over our fragile Polish state, with many in our cabinet revisiting Czartoryski's ideas and suggesting that we claim East Prussia to completely secure what otherwise would be an exclave that Prussia would be keen on connecting with.
Renounce National Egoism

§GLose 5 infamy§! §RThis will remove German cores on Poznan and Thorn!§! When the Poznan Question was brought into the Frankfurt Parliament, Polish Independence was still supported by many representatives that tried to reconcile German and Polish Nationalism. As our attempts to divide Poznan failed to find a settlement that wouldn't leave neither Germans or Germans stranded outside or their territory, those ideas would be ultimately killed by Wilhelm Jordan's speech, where the liberal parliamentarian would paint the  "sentimental Polonophilia" of some liberals as a childish fantasy that had no right to get in the way of a unified Germany and its future, stating that the Germans should practice "healthy national egoism" and abandon any sympathy for the Polish cause within Poznan. Jordan's speech would erode most support for Polish independence within the German liberals, with only radicals like Robert Blum insisting on advocating for an independent Poland. \n\nNow, while we did promote a violent crackdown in Greater Poland after our cooperation with the Polish became unsustainable, many of the radical liberal and socialist elements in our country still favorably see Polish independence and there are still some that see the establishment of Poland as pivotal to curtail a future Russian threat. While it obviously would be madness to establish a Polish state in Poznan, if we were to conquer Warsaw we could leverage the liberal and socialist members of our parliament to renounce our national egoism and push a semi-independent Polish nation that would—under our supervision—fulfill both the interests of both the German and Polish peoples.
